reviews.title: I would not be staying here again
reviews.positives: Beds were comfortable.
reviews.negatives:
Disappointed with Customer Service and Lack of Transparency
What I didn’t like was the poor customer service, especially at check-in. The agent was very short with me, didn’t explain any of the charges made to my card, and rushed me—even though there was no one else waiting. When I asked to upgrade my room, I was told the hotel was fully booked, yet I was given a room on the 12th floor with a terrible ceiling view, despite my booking stating a city view. I asked again about upgrading to a suite, but I was brushed off.

The next day, my card was charged an extra $200 without explanation. When I arrived, valet parking was full, so I was told to park across the street and was assured my parking would be validated. However, the next day, the check-in agent only gave me an $8 discount out of a $30 charge—and again, didn’t explain the charges. I only realized what happened when I saw my card statement and had to figure it out myself.

At checkout, I asked about the charges and was again brushed off. I had already been charged, and then another $200 was added with no clear explanation. The staff didn’t seem welcoming or concerned, which was frustrating considering I spent over $1,000 for just two nights.

The hotel really needs to work on its customer service. First impressions matter, and unfortunately, this one fell short. Definitely not worth the price.

reviews.title: The delivery of the package to the room is too late...
reviews.text: I had planned to stay at an Airbnb because I have a child, but since I couldn't stay for one night, I used this hotel on the day of the Dodgers game. I spotted a few Japanese people who seemed to be attending the game as well. It was a rental car, but there was parking in the basement that allowed us to enter the hotel without walking down any suspicious streets. I checked in at 3 PM, and when I returned to the hotel around 10 PM, my suitcase still hadn't arrived in the room (I was told it would be delivered before my arrival). I couldn't reach the room by phone, so I had to go directly to the reception to confirm. After waiting, it finally arrived just before midnight. I was terrified to think what would have happened if I hadn't brought enough milk and diapers for the baby. Since it was just one night, there were no other particular issues. I was able to borrow a baby crib and extra pillows. To be honest, it was a stay that I could overlook because it was just one night.

reviews.title: Hotel from hell
reviews.positives: The location is great
reviews.negatives: This place is the absolute worst. Whenever I travel to LA, I stay at this hotel, and I thought I found the perfect place to stay, but I am currently having the most nightmare vacation. I was charged twice for the hotel & when I contacted them to get this resolved, they said there would be no one to help me until Monday. It is currently Friday, & I am out the money that I planned to use for my vacation. So now I am stranded at this horrid hotel low on funds for my “vacation.” The front desk gave me a money voucher for $200 for my group of 3 for a 4 day stay. When I went to use the voucher for some snacks at the cafe, I was screamed at that the voucher could only be used once. The cafe manager broke the voucher into 4 $50 vouchers. Since I was just planning on getting some snacks & not planning on spending $50 out of the $200 I was given, I went back to front desk. I was sure that this was some kind of mistake because why would I be given 1 $200 voucher if it could only be used once. They let me know that this isn’t a mistake & that there was no other solution. I feel like I’m in some kind of horrible Twilight Zone episode where I wandered into a hotel from hell. Also, the room is absolutely disgusting! I have never had an issue at this hotel, (typically using the blue elevator) but this time the room is COVERED in dust (used the yellow elevator). It’s like no one has cleaned it in months! My group has been coughing constantly because it’s so dusty. Unfortunately, I have booked this hotel in advance for a trip next weekend & will have to return to this nightmare. I can’t imagine booking this hotel again, as it is the most frustrating, annoying trip of my life. I need a vacation from this hotel!

reviews.title: Hotel is dishonest does not care about its guests
reviews.text:
We had a serious of events lead us to having a poor experience at this hotel: later checkout offered complimentary incorrectly processed. We lost our keys and had to get them to cut the locks off our bags. In which we were happy to verify the bags were ours, which was disregarded by lock cutter, then an hour later security came in with a sniffer dog claiming to audit rooms twice a year. My husband asked if this was due to them having to cut the locks off, we were told this was random and everyone in the floor was being checked. I emailed and got a response from a Stephanie who said the hotel does this 3 times a week as they have a lot of people staying from all over. 

Even if hotel as part of security purposes needed to ensure there was nothing in our bags that was suspicious, they could have asked to see proof of bags and advised they would need to have a dog smell them instead of doing the later afterwards.

This made us feel like we weren’t welcome in the hotel. 

Hotel could have been more humanistic and understood that sometimes people misplace things, like they misplaced our complimentary checkout...

reviews.title: Hotel is fine, staff are rude and unhelpful
reviews.positives: Location is convenient. Close to downtown.
reviews.negatives:
The staff at the property are very rude. My room was booked for 2 adults. I called to add my husband’s name to the reservation and they refused to add it to the reservation. Staff (Tom) insisted I contact booking.com to add the name. I did. I called back and the “Manager” (Francisco) refused to add the name and was dismissive and wouldn’t listen that I had contacted booking.com and had an email confirmation with the added name. He also insisted that it was a problem that didn’t need to be addressed since my reservation was for the next day. *Side note: He also tried to claim that he didn’t have any information regarding my reservation even though I had given my name and he knew when my reservation was for. Total gaslighting behavior.*

I tried to explain that I would be getting on a flight in a few hours, would not be able to contact the hotel later, and that my husband would be arriving before me. He continued to be rude and dismissive. When I told him that I felt that he was being rude, rather than toning down his attitude, he accused me of being rude because I had expressed my distaste for his unprofessionalism. The conversation went nowhere. Francisco was not helpful and had no care that he was exhibiting terrible customer service. I ended the call feeling frustrated. When my husband arrived at the hotel, his name was not added to the reservation. Ironically, Francisco was at the front desk and became quite flustered when my husband presented the email confirmation for the booking as well as the confirmation which added his name to the reservation. 

It should not be so difficult to add the second guest to a reservation for 2. Staff at this hotel should be more accommodating and less sassy to guests. If they don’t want to be hospitable, they should not work in hospitality. Listening to customers instead of cutting them off or rushing them off goes a long way.

reviews.negatives: The room was terrible. Air conditioner was so old and made so much noise we had to keep it off although it was quite cold. Also, because the room is so small and the machine is placed so low on a wall, it blows directly on the people lying on one of the beds. The beds are not full size beds. They are definitely small for 2 people. The room is small. The bathroom is outdated and the ventilation does not work, and the ventilation hole is full of mould, for which I have pictures. Only valet parking is available, you cannot park your own car, you have to give away the keys and the experience is very inconvenient and strange. The hotel, in general, has seen better days.  We walked around and the restaurants and shops in the 3rd and 4th floors were mostly closed. Only one place, a bar, and a small convenience store, not affiliated with the hotel and with high prices was open. Considering that there are no other shops to buy water etc. close to the hotel, this is also very inconvenient. We had to wait 30 minutes to check-in, and there was a line of 10 people waiting before us with one person working at the check-in counter on a Saturday at 16:30. I would recommend everyone, especially families with children, to stay away from this hotel.

reviews.title: 가격대비 정말 별로 였습니다.
reviews.text:
호텔외관만 좋아서 예약했는데 내부 객실은 정말 별로였습니다.
이곳에 묵었을때 내내 비가와서 히터를 틀었더니 소리가 엄청 시끄러워서 그냥 끄고 잤습니다. 이상한 냄새도 나더라구요.  그래서 히터를 안틀고 잤더니 너무 추워서 감기에 걸려서 왔습니다. 화장실, 객실 너무 좁아서 성인 3명이서 지내는데 너무 불편했습니다. 직원들도 불친절했고 주차비도 너무 비쌌습니다. 45불?47불 이었는데 정말 가격대비 숙소가 너무 별로였습니다. 
위치도 너무 다운타운이라 위험하고 낮에도 으스스하고 무서웠습니다. 
정말 외관만 보고 예약했는데 돈이 아깝네요 ~

During my stay, Only the housekeeping staff were very kind.

I will never recommend this stupid hotel.

reviews.title: 가성비 좋은호텔
reviews.text:
2박 후기

장점- 가성비가 좋음, 침구류가 편안하고 깨끗함, 직원들이 친절하고 1층 카페가 유용함.
주변에 그랜드센트럴마켓 과 더브로드 등 관광지가 가까운편.

단점- 방에 욕실가운 및 슬리퍼 비치안됨, 호텔 안내가 전혀 없었음, 건물내에 식당가가 크게 있는데 오후 3시면 전부 문닫음 (사실상 이용불가), 수압이 약함,
청소하러 8시에 매일와서 깨움, 방음이 잘 안됨

총평- 불편했던 점들이 큰 문제는 아니었고, 다운타운에 묵어야 한다면 재방문의사가 있음.

reviews.title: I expected more from a Marriott.
reviews.positives: The location is good, the hotel and rooms are nice, and the pool is big and deep.
reviews.negatives: $57 to park is absolutely ridiculous. The lounge chairs in the pool area are pretty gross. The brewery is kind of a joke. It’s a restaurant with a few beers and some alcohol. The worst part was the check-in process. The line was a mile long, and the front desk lady was rude and obviously had no idea what she was doing. First, she gave me an OCCUPIED room that I almost walked into. I had to go back down to the lobby just to be assigned another room and go back up 30 floors where the keys to the new room didn’t work. The nice housekeepers called security to let me in my room, but after 15 minutes of waiting, I went back down to the lobby and asked a manager to fix the keys. I told him about my experience and the rude, untrained front desk worker, and he said “I’m sorry about that” and fixed my keys. Unbelievable for $200 a night!!

The front desk is helpful and friendly. But the rooms assigned are awful although it's side by side with my other family that's the only goodthing.
reviews.negatives:
The room assigned to us are not great, not a 4 star room. Carpet in the room old and looks like not been vacuum, got hives when I got home Toronto. The fridge wasn't been cleaned, old bottles of drinks still inside, we don't bother to report to front desk as we will stay for 2 days only. The blanket on top was so thin and if Ac on you will get freezing to cold. Comforter you will see in the 4 star hotel not provided. Lastly, when we first got into the room smelled like a smoking room and not a non-smoking one, got allergies started so I just sprayed the room with my perfume.
Only the washroom is cleaned, the towels look better and the washroom kit is reasonable.
Valet Parking have to ready 49 us dollars plus tax no choice of self parking.
Overall stay, not happy.
